Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The result says `gameplay_changed: false` and `no_impact: true`. This means the RIGHT actions didn't change the gameplay, only the HUD. Let me check the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'c', 'W', 'G', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}), children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player DID move! Let me compare:
- Before: player at (28,20)-(30,23), white strip at (31,20)-(35,23)
- After: player at (28,40)-(30,43), white strip at (31,40)-(35,43)
The player moved from cols 20-23 to cols 40-43. The white strip moved with the player.
The merged object is at (24,44)-(31,47). The player is now at (28,40)-(30,43), which is just to the left of the merged object.
The white strip is at (31,40)-(35,43), which is below the player and to the left of the merged object.
Now I need to merge the carried square (in the white strip) with the merged object. Looking at the positions:
- Merged object: (24,44)-(31,47)
- White strip: (31,40)-(35,43)
The white strip is at rows 31-35, cols 40-43. The merged object is at rows 24-31, cols 44-47.
They're adjacent at the corner: white strip's top-right is (31,43), merged object's bottom-left is (31,44).
Let me try moving RIGHT to push the white strip into the merged object.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at (28,40)-(30,43), merged object at (24,44)-(31,47)
# White strip at (31,40)-(35,43)
# Move RIGHT to merge white strip with merged object
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
